title: phyllodoce glanduliflora var. intermedia
intro: Phyllodoce glanduliflora var. intermedia, also known as the glandular mountainheath or mountain azalea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and it is also believed to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Phyllodoce glanduliflora var. intermedia include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be dried and used as a herbal powder or added to salves and ointments for topical use.
